campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help pay for youth recreation scholarships. The council also discussed extending a temporary local rule and moving forward with planning for a new bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation to youth scholarship fund from Water Tower Kitchen
Extending temporary local ordinance #2326
Planning bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Submitting grant application for Hamilton Avenue overcrossing project

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or law they're considering. After hearing from residents, the council moved forward by introducing the ordinance and skipping the normal first reading step, planning to vote on it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback on a proposed ordinance
Council waived the first reading requirement to speed up the process
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next regular council meeting
Fast-track approval process being used for this item

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from various March sessions and authorize the town manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda was primarily administrative, focused on formalizing previous meeting records and moving forward with a construction project.
keyTopics: Approval of March meeting minutes from multiple sessions
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Closed session discussions (specific topics not detailed in agenda)

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 15, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize and finalize their upcoming agendas for April 21st and April 28th, 2026. They reviewed what items should be added to each meeting, set specific times for certain discussions, and checked that proper public notice requirements were met. The council also reviewed public records from early April and discussed VEBA (a benefits program for city employees).
keyTopics: Finalized the April 21, 2026 council meeting agenda
Planned items for the April 28, 2026 draft agenda
Set specific time slots for agenda items to keep meetings on schedule
Reviewed public notices and document filing requirements
Discussed VEBA Advisory Committee matters

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 14, 2026, where they reviewed the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year and discussed upcoming city projects and improvements. The council also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and community volunteers in Mountain View.
keyTopics: 2026-27 Capital Improvement Program budget review
Teen Month proclamation
National Volunteer Week recognition
City infrastructure and project planning

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, where they opened with the Pledge of Allegiance and highlighted two important observances: Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month. These special recognitions were part of the council's agenda to celebrate community diversity and promote fair housing practices in the city.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
Community diversity initiatives
City council opening ceremonies

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held a community workshop to help residents understand and use OpenGov, a tool that shows where Cupertino's money comes from and where it goes. The council also reviewed rules requiring people who lobby the city about decisions to register and report their activities.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool demonstration
City budget information for residents
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements
How to track Cupertino's spending

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council meeting covered important legal information about how residents can challenge city decisions. If you disagree with a city ruling, you have 90 days from when the decision is final to file a lawsuit or legal challenge, or you'll lose your right to do so.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to challenge city decisions in court
Legal disclosure requirements for City Council meetings
Rules governing how residents can appeal city rulings

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026, to handle several administrative matters including updating the city's federal funding plan for 2025-2030 and reviewing new project requests from council members. They also considered changing the rules for how council vacancies are filled and approved meeting minutes from previous sessions.
keyTopics: Updated federal funding plan (2025-2030 Consolidated Plan) for city programs
New project requests submitted by individual council members
Changes to how city council fills vacant seats
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a proposal to upgrade the historic status of a building at 405 Kipling Street downtown, which would allow the owner to add 5,000 square feet of new space as a reward for preserving and improving the historic structure and making it safer against earthquakes. The council also approved meeting minutes from their Historic Resources Board's previous session.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street building status upgrade from Category 4 to Category 2 historic designation
5,000 square feet of bonus floor area approved for seismic safety improvements
Downtown commercial district development with historic preservation incentives
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and assignments
